ace: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Acehnese.
acre: noun. An English unit of land area (symbol: a. or ac.) originally denoting a day 's ploughing for a yoke of oxen, now standardized as 4,840
aero: adjective. Of or pertaining to aviation.
age: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Angal.
are: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Western Arrernte.
ave: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Avestan.
aver: verb. To assert the truth of (something); to affirm (something) with confidence; to declare (something) in a positive manner.
avo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Agavotaguerra.
cage: noun. An enclosure made of spaced bars, normally to hold animals while providing some ventilation.
cager: noun. A person or machine responsible for managing a mineshaft cage.
car: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kari'na.
cargo: noun. Freight carried by a ship, aircraft, or motor vehicle.
cave: noun. A large, naturally-occurring cavity formed underground or in the face of a cliff or a hillside.
caver: noun. A person who explores caves.
cere: noun. A fleshy, waxy area at the base of the upper beak in certain birds.
cero: noun. A large scombroid food fish (Scomberomorus regalis) found chiefly in the West Indies.
cog: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chong.
cor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Cornish.
core: noun. In general usage, an essential part of a thing surrounded by other essential things.
corvee: noun. Unpaid labor required by a feudal lord.
crag: noun. A rocky outcrop; a rugged steep cliff or rock.
crave: verb. To desire strongly, so as to satisfy an appetite; to long or yearn for.
eager: adjective. Desirous; keen to do or obtain something.
ear: noun. The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna or auricle, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
ego: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eggon.
era: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eravallan.
erg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Sie.
gae: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Warekena.
gar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Galeya.
gear: noun. Equipment or paraphernalia, especially that used for an athletic endeavor.
gee: interjection. A general exclamation of surprise or frustration.
goa: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Guro.
goer: noun. One who, or that which, goes.
gor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gorontalo.
gore: noun. Blood, especially that from a wound when thickened due to exposure to the air.
grave: noun. An excavation in the earth as a place of burial.
greave: noun. A bush; a tree; a grove.
gree: noun. One of a flight of steps.
oar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Old Aramaic.
oca: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ocaina.
ocrea: noun. A sheath around a plant stem forming from the stipule of a leaf and extending above the point of insertion of the leaf.
ora: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Oroha.
orc: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orma.
orca: noun. A sea mammal (Orcinus orca) related to dolphins and porpoises, commonly called the killer whale.
ore: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orejón.
overage: adjective. Having an age that is greater than a stipulated minimum.
race: noun. A contest between people, animals, vehicles, etc. where the goal is to be the first to reach some objective.
rave: noun. An enthusiastic review (such as of a play).
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
reg: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Kara (Tanzania).
vac: noun. Clipping of vacation (“extended period of time away from work or school”).
var: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Huarijio.
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
veer: verb. To let out (a sail-line), to allow (a sheet) to run out.
voe: noun. A narrow sea inlet, particularly in the Shetland Islands, similar to a fjord.
